title: Embedded with Starmer: After me, the resignation
gist: A satirical set-piece on Keir Starmer's resignation day, skewering him as an unpopular leader who seized control then did little, abandoned his 2020 pledges, and is displaced by an activist Andy Burnham amid a media culture that prizes commentary over governance.

insights.claim: The satire frames Keir Starmer resigning as one of the most loathed men in the country, a leader who insisted on taking control and then didn't do very much, purging socialism from Labour rather than delivering change.
insights.speaker: Henry Morris
insights.so_what: Captures the prevailing narrative of Starmer's premiership as inertia—context for a leadership transition.

insights.claim: Starmer's 2020 Labour leadership campaign pledges—raising income tax on the top 5%, reversing corporation tax cuts, abolishing Universal Credit and supporting public ownership of utilities and transport—were abandoned in office.
insights.speaker: Henry Morris
insights.so_what: A reminder of the gap between Labour's stated 2020 platform and its record.
insights.locator: Mandelson’s latest withdrawal has left a single folder

insights.claim: Andy Burnham is portrayed as an activist successor who 'wants to do things', contrasted against Starmer's preference for not upsetting vested interests, and is drawing intense media attention as he arrives at Euston.
insights.speaker: Henry Morris
insights.so_what: Signals a Labour pivot toward a more interventionist leader—watch for policy-direction change.
insights.locator: “He wants to do things,” complains Starmer

insights.claim: The piece's thesis is that in British politics 'government is only ever a temporary interruption to the more important business of commentary'.
insights.speaker: Henry Morris
insights.so_what: A cynical read on the primacy of media narrative over governance in shaping political outcomes.
insights.quote: government is only ever a temporary interruption to the more important business of commentary
insights.locator: The cameras have already turned away
